senses.definition: a reward in some video games that is obtained after completing specific, often challenging tasks
an image that is the representation of a player in a game or an account on social media

examples.x: She created a colorful avatar to represent her in the online game.
His avatar in the virtual world looked just like him, with similar clothes and hairstyle.
Players can customize their avatar with different outfits and accessories.
The avatar danced around the screen during the interactive party.
She chose a cute animal as her avatar for the social media platform.

senses.definition: (of a character in a video game) to hide or stay in one spot for an extended period of time in order to remain safe or to ambush other players

examples.x: In the multiplayer shooter game, the sniper decided to camp on the high ground, picking off enemies from a distance.
During the final moments of the battle royale match, some players choose to camp in strategic locations to increase their chances of survival.
The defensive team decided to camp near the bomb site, setting up barricades and traps to thwart the attacking team's advances.

senses.definition: a feature in some computer games that enables the participants across the world to play a game together regardless of having different platforms, operating systems, or consoles

examples.x: I didn’t think cross-play would work so smoothly, but it’s really fun playing with people on different systems.
I wish more games offered cross-play; it would make finding players a lot easier.
Thanks to cross-play, I no longer have to worry about what platform my friends are using to join in the game.
It’s great that this game allows cross-play because now I can play with my cousin, even though he's using a different platform.

senses.definition: a non-interactive scene in a video game that is usually shown when the player levels up, dies, or reaches another chapter of the game to develop the plot of the game

examples.x: The game had an exciting cutscene right before the final boss battle.
I was so engrossed in the cutscene that I almost forgot to pick up the controller.
The cutscene at the beginning of the game gave a great introduction to the story.
After the intense battle, the cutscene explained what would happen next.
We skipped the cutscene because we had already seen it during our last playthrough.

senses.definition: a message, reference, or feature that is intentionally hidden by the directors of a movie, developers of a game, or writers of a book, etc. for the sake of amusement
a point awarded to a player who has passed a level, accomplished a mission, collected a particular item, or defeated a specific enemy in a computer game

examples.x: She gained 100 experience points after defeating the boss in the game.
The team worked together and gained a lot of experience points from the quest.

senses.definition: to kill an enemy in a shooting game
the practice of devoting long hours into playing a specific game in order to collect in-game currency and related assets and make money by selling them to other players

examples.x: He spent hours gold farming in the game to buy better equipment for his character.
Many players dislike gold farming because it can make the game feel unfair.
Gold farming has become a business for some players who want to make money from the game.
After a few weeks of gold farming, she had enough currency to buy the rarest items in the game.

senses.definition: (in computer games) an area that the player has access to as they take on an objective
(of a player's character) to advance to the next level in terms of strength, ability, etc.

examples.x: The warrior leveled up after defeating the dragon, gaining new combat skills and increased strength.
The mage leveled up after completing the magical quest, expanding their knowledge of spells and enhancing their mana pool.
The thief leveled up after mastering the art of pickpocketing, becoming more adept at stealing valuable items undetected.

senses.definition: the fact or act of completely defeating a rival or enemy in a video game

examples.x: I can't believe how much pwnage happened during that final round, it was like he was untouchable.
She was on fire today, just pwnage after pwnage.
That was some serious pwnage, I thought I had a chance, but then he wiped the floor with me.
The way he took out that boss without breaking a sweat was pure pwnage.

senses.definition: to completely defeat a rival or opponent, particularly in a video game
(particularly of a computer game or music) worthy of playing more than once

examples.x: This game has great replayability because there are so many different ways to complete the levels.
The song has a lot of replayability; I keep finding new details every time I listen to it.
The game's replayability is what keeps me playing it even after finishing the main story.
I love listening to this album; it has incredible replayability, and I never tire of the songs.
The movie wasn't that good, but it has replayability since I can watch it over and over without getting bored.

senses.definition: a quality in video games that makes them worth experiencing more than once
(of a video game character) to come back to life after having been killed
(of a computer game) involving a lot of shooting and bloodshed
someone who plays video games
an electronic device used for playing video games on a television or display screen

examples.x: He bought a new console to enjoy the latest video games.
She connected the console to the television for a better gaming experience.
Consoles often come with wireless controllers for ease of use.
They spent the weekend playing multiplayer games on their consoles.

senses.definition: a high-quality, high-budget video game developed by a major company with extensive resources

examples.x: I spent the entire weekend playing the latest triple-A release.
I can’t wait to play the latest triple-A that just came out this week.

senses.definition: an internet and gaming term used to indicate that a person is temporarily not available or not actively participating in an online activity or game because they are not physically present at their computer or keyboard
the range or radius of an action or ability that affects multiple targets within a specified area in video games or role-playing games

examples.x: The wizard's fireball spell has a large area of effect, damaging all the enemies in the area.
The game's new update introduced more abilities with a larger area of effect.
Be careful when you cast that ability, it has an area of effect that could hit your teammates.

senses.definition: enemies or characters that have an excessive amount of health or resilience, often requiring players to expend a large number of bullets or attacks to defeat them

examples.x: I got frustrated when the enemy turned out to be a bullet sponge, constantly dodging my shots and not going down.
That mini-boss felt like a bullet sponge; I emptied almost an entire clip into it before it finally fell.
The game developers made the final level harder by turning the enemies into bullet sponges.
The enemy's armor made him a bullet sponge, and it took forever to break through.
I don't mind challenging fights, but sometimes the bullet sponge enemies just feel like they're wasting my time.

senses.definition: a gaming term used to describe the strategy of staying in one location or area for an extended period, often to gain an advantage or to ambush opponents
the act of using tactics or strategies in a video game that exploit weaknesses or loopholes in the game mechanics to gain an advantage over opponents
an environment visualized or created, especially in a video game
the process of incorporating game-like elements and mechanics, such as points, rewards, leaderboards, challenges, and progress tracking, into non-game contexts

examples.x: Many fitness apps use gamification to motivate users, like earning badges for reaching goals.
Schools are starting to incorporate gamification into lessons to make learning more engaging for students.
The app uses gamification to keep users interested by offering challenges and rewards for progress.

senses.definition: the act of playing video games or other interactive media, typically on a computer, console, or mobile device, for entertainment, recreation, or competition
a virtual item in a video game that can be purchased with real money or in-game currency and contains random rewards, such as virtual items, currency, or other in-game bonuses

a small financial transaction typically made within a video game or other software application, often using real-world currency or virtual currency, for the purpose of acquiring virtual goods or other in-game benefits

examples.x: I spent a few dollars on a microtransaction to unlock a new character in the game.
After a few microtransactions, I had unlocked most of the cool skins in the game.
Microtransactions in mobile games often include options for buying in-game currency or extra lives.
I didn’t want to buy any microtransactions, but the limited-time offer was too tempting.

senses.definition: a person who spends excessive amounts of time using a computer, particularly for activities such as browsing the internet, playing video games, or engaging in social media
(of a computer system or software application) designed for use by more than one person at the same time
the act of reducing the power or effectiveness of a weapon, ability, or strategy in order to balance or improve gameplay

examples.x: The latest update includes a nerf to the sniper rifle, making it less powerful.
Players were upset when the developers announced a nerf to their favorite character.
After the nerf, the healing ability was no longer as effective during battles.
Many fans complained about the nerf, saying it ruined their playstyle.
The recent nerf to the armor system made it harder to survive longer fights.

senses.definition: a temporary or permanent enhancement to a character's abilities, attributes, or performance, often granted by items, power-ups, or other in-game mechanisms
a visual design or appearance that can be applied to a character, weapon, or other in-game object to change its look without affecting its performance or gameplay mechanics
a group of players or individuals who come together to form a community or team in video games, typically to compete together or achieve common goals
a game mechanic in which players can create or upgrade items, equipment, or other in-game assets using various resources or components
additional digital content or expansions for a video game that can be purchased and downloaded after the initial release to enhance the gaming experience

examples.x: The game just released some downloadable content, which includes new characters and maps.
The downloadable content for the game was a great way to add new challenges.
The game has a lot of downloadable content that adds new characters and missions.

senses.definition: a metric used in video games to measure the amount of damage a character or weapon can deal over a specific period, usually one second. It helps players understand the efficiency and effectiveness of their attacks in combat situations
to ambush or attack an opponent, often by surprise or with a numerical advantage, in a video game
an expression used in the gaming community to show sportsmanship and respect to opponents after a match or game has concluded
a statistical measure that represents the number of kills a player achieves in relation to the number of times they have been killed
the action of repeatedly and rapidly pressing buttons on a controller, often without a specific strategy or timing, in order to perform actions in a video game

examples.x: His button mashing got him through the first few levels, but he quickly ran into trouble on the harder stages.
The game rewarded skillful play, not just button mashing, so players had to learn the right moves.
He relied too much on button mashing, which made him lose the match.
Button mashing became his go-to method when he got frustrated with the complex controls.
Her button mashing was a bit chaotic, but it worked well enough to defeat the boss.

senses.definition: a resource used in some video games to cast spells or perform special abilities by consuming a specific amount of magical energy or power
a computer user, particularly a video gamer, who does not have the needed knowledge or skills to have a good performance

examples.x: The seasoned gamers teased the noob for his lack of experience in the game.
As a noob to the world of coding, she sought guidance from more experienced programmers.
The noob mistakenly activated a trap in the game, much to the amusement of his teammates.
The noob's questions about the game's controls revealed his unfamiliarity with the genre.
He laughed off the insults from other players, knowing that everyone starts as a noob at some point.

senses.definition: referring to a game element, character, or ability that is excessively strong or dominant, often giving an unfair advantage compared to other elements or players
a term used to describe a game mode or situation in which players compete directly against each other, rather than against computer-controlled opponents

examples.x: I prefer player versus player modes because they are more challenging than playing against the computer.
The game has a dedicated player versus player arena where you can test your skills against others.

senses.definition: a gameplay mechanic in video games that requires the player to press specific buttons or perform actions in response to on-screen prompts within a limited timeframe to progress through the game or perform certain actions

examples.x: He failed the quick time event because he was not paying attention to the screen.
Some players dislike quick time events because they interrupt the flow of gameplay.

senses.definition: to abruptly quit a video game out of frustration or anger, often by intentionally disconnecting from the game or leaving the match
any character in a video game that is controlled by the computer itself rather than the playe

examples.x: That non-player character keeps repeating the same line every time I walk past him.
The friendly non-player character guided me through the tutorial and explained how to use my abilities.

senses.definition: the act of an experienced player using a new or alternate account to play against lower-skilled opponents, typically to achieve easy wins or exploit the matchmaking system

examples.x: Smurfing ruins the experience for beginners who just want to learn and enjoy the game.
The developers added stricter rules to discourage smurfing in competitive matches.
Smurfing often leads to unbalanced games, frustrating both newer players and those trying to climb the ranks.
Smurfing has become a problem in many online games, as it creates an unfair advantage.

senses.definition: a character or role in a game that has high durability and is designed to absorb damage and protect other members of the team
a player who prioritizes maximizing their character's power and effectiveness in the game

examples.x: He’s such a power gamer; he finished the game’s hardest level in just a few hours.
As a power gamer, she spends most of her free time improving her skills and collecting rare items.
The power gamer in our group always knows the best strategies to win quickly.
He doesn’t just play for fun, he’s a power gamer, always aiming to reach the top rank.
As a power gamer, he had the best gear in the game before anyone else could catch up.

senses.definition: the numerical representation of a player's or character's health or vitality, which determines their ability to withstand damage or survive in the game
a type of interactive audio in video games that dynamically changes based on the player's actions or the game's events to enhance the gaming experience

examples.x: The adaptive music in that game really helped set the mood for each scene, from peaceful moments to high-stakes action.
I loved how the adaptive music calmed down when I completed a tough mission and entered a safe zone.
The adaptive music smoothly transitioned from light and upbeat to dark and ominous as the plot unfolded.

senses.definition: a fictional character in a game, whether in a tabletop role-playing game or a video game, that is controlled and guided by a player throughout the course of the game's events and challenges

examples.x: I spent hours designing my player character before starting the game.
In this RPG, your player character's decisions shape the entire storyline.
My player character keeps dying in the same spot; I need to improve my strategy.
The player character interacts with NPCs to gather information and complete quests.
As the game progresses, your player character gains new abilities and stronger stats.

senses.definition: the best or highest achievement in terms of points or performance in a game
to play computer or video games

examples.x: During the weekend, I like to game with my siblings and compete in multiplayer matches.
He prefers to game on his PC because of the superior graphics and performance.
My friends and I often game late into the night, immersed in virtual worlds and epic battles.
